text: Pericardium was adherent to the right atrium, aorta, and right ventricle.
There was a cystic lesion inside the pericardium over the left and right ventricle.
Pericardial additions were released over the aorta, right atrium, and part of right ventricle.
Patient was hyperanized for adequate ACT.
Cardiopulmonary bypass was initiated via aortic, SVC and IVC cannulation.
Hypertonic saline soaked sponges were placed around the cyst.
On pump beating heart under transesophageal echocardiography guidance, the pericyst was incised.
The daughter cysts were expelled out with each heartbeat.
The daughter cysts were completely sucked out of the pericyst using a wall suction.
Entrop-TEE still showed a lobulated cyst inside the right ventricle.
So the heart was arrested using anticoagulant cardioplegia after applying the aortic cross clamp.
Right atrium was opened and the right ventricle was inspected.
There was a small cyst near the capillary muscle.
It was excised.
After the excision, there was a communication noted between the right ventricle and the pericyst.
The opening of the communication was closed on the right ventricle with parolin sutures.
The right atrium was closed in two layers.
The heart was filled with blood and there was no filling in the pericyst.
The excessive pericyst was excised and the remaining pericyst was closed in a double-breasted fashion over the left ventricle.
Patient was made of bypass smoothly.
Interruptive E showed no residual intraventricular cysts.
